title: Classify and retire stale Docker development residues
goal: Classify the remaining stopped or never-started Heim-PC Docker development containers and their persistent volumes against current repository, Compose, proof and recovery ownership, then remove only terminal residue through bounded container- and volume-specific actions.

approval.note: Task registration and read-only classification do not authorize Docker effects. Any container, volume or network removal requires a fresh exact-ID plan, current ownership and lease readback, rollback or export sufficiency evidence, and break-glass approval bound to that reviewed effect.

acceptance.assertion: Each remaining candidate container and attached volume is re-read live and classified as active-source, restartable-development-state, retained-proof, terminal-container-only, terminal-volume or unknown-owner using Compose labels, mounts, current repo activity, leases and receipts.
No running container, active Compose project, current worktree bind mount, unresolved proof database, leased resource or volume without a verified owner/disposition is removed.

Every approved cleanup names exact container and volume IDs; container-only cleanup never implies volume deletion, and persistent volume removal requires separate export or explicit dispensability evidence.
After effects, Docker container, volume and network state plus the active Heim utility and Weltgewebe-related containers are re-read; no intended service is degraded.
Retained candidates record a concrete owner and next disposition; removed candidates record before/after IDs and whether any volume was intentionally preserved.

safe_cleanup_already_applied.name: heim-util-beszel-agent-prepin-20260710T215036Z
safe_cleanup_already_applied.reason: Exited replacement candidate with only bind mounts; active pinned heim-util-beszel-agent remained running.
